title: Go HTTP Upload and Download Gateways · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds streaming Go file-gateway handler, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of streaming Go file-gateway handler while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply trusted authority, authentication, authorization, method, route, headers, body, multipart, rate, and concurrency admission before storage work.
Use MaxBytesReader or bounded readers and streaming multipart iteration without ParseMultipartForm disk or memory surprises.
Delay response commitment until safe, set representation headers, stream bounded data, and record partial-write terminal states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Http Request Admission
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 2608 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a streaming Go file handler on request admission and goroutine cleanup; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Http Request Admission.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply trusted authority, authentication, authorization, method, route, headers, body, multipart, rate, and concurrency admission before storage work. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Http Request Admission, keep Http Request Admission connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Gateway Abuse Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: storage-go-gateway-abuse-defense-15ni6a" that uses case 3815 to gate a streaming Go file handler on request admission and goroutine cleanup.
